mission.title: meshery: [UI] Navigator Active Tab Enhancement
mission.description: [UI] Navigator Active Tab Enhancement. Requested by 1+ users.

steps.title: Check current meshery deployment
steps.description:
Verify your meshery version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/name=meshery
helm list -n meshery 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working meshery installation.

steps.title: Review meshery configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant meshery configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/name=meshery
kubectl get configmap -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=meshery
```
#### Current Behavior
Currently, The active tab is not highlighted/representing that it is active/opened. 

#### Desired Behavior
Let's have it highlighted to represent that the particular tab is active/opened.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [UI] Navigator Active Tab Enhancement
steps.description:
highlighted tab to represent that the particular tab is active/opened.
This PR fixes #5715

Always open for suggestions and improvements. Please point me out if anything should be added/corrected.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n meshery -l app.kubernetes.io/name=meshery
kubectl get events -n meshery --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[UI] Navigator Active Tab Enhancement" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
highlighted tab to represent that the particular tab is active/opened.
This PR fixes #5715

Always open for suggestions and improvements. Please point me out if anything should be added/corrected.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with meshery installed or the issue environment reproducible.
